analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet asserts that China must stand firm against "unfriendly forces," respond to defamation and attacks, and speak up for justice against power politics and bullying; it frames this stance as a duty of Chinese diplomacy and a trait of the Chinese people. Evaluation: CH1 (The West is immoral, hostile and decadent) – Implicitly supported: references to "unfriendly forces," "defamations," "power politics & bullying" suggest hostile others targeting China. While not naming the West/US, this aligns with the familiar framing of external (often Western) hostility toward China; thus CH1 is promoted in generic form. CH2 (China is a benevolent power) – Partially supported: the tweet claims obligation to "speak up for justice," positioning China as defending justice internationally; this matches the benevolent/justice-defending posture, though without details on multilateralism or development. CH3 (epic history) – Not present; no historical references. CH4 (appropriate political system/values) – Weak/no: it mentions national character and diplomacy but does not explicitly advocate China’s political system or domestic values as superior; focus is external stance. CH5 (CCP succeeds) – Not present; no governance performance claims. CH6 (culture/nature/heritage) – Not relevant. Conclusion: The tweet promotes CH1 (hostile others vs China) and CH2 (China speaks up for justice).
